title: elaeocarpus lepidus
intro: Elaeocarpus lepidus is a species of flowering plant in the Elaeocarpaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. It is also known as the "blue quandong" or "hairy blue olive tree." Elaeocarpus lepidus is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's fruit, leaves, and bark can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function.
